records.ety: [Cf. Icel. beysta to strike, powder; Sw. basa to beat with a rod: perh. akin to E. beat.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Basted; p. pr. & vb. n. Basting.]

defs: To beat with a stick; to cudgel.
quotes: One man was basted by the keeper for carrying some people over on his back through the waters.

defs: To sprinkle flour and salt and drip butter or fat on, as on meat in roasting.
To mark with tar, as sheep.

records.ety: [OE. basten, OF. bastir, F. btir, prob. fr. OHG. bestan to sew, MHG. besten to bind, fr. OHG. bast bast. See Bast.]

defs: To sew loosely, or with long stitches; -- usually, that the work may be held in position until sewed more firmly.
